sentences.sentence: The idea that the continents are moving was first proposed by a German meteorologist,.Alfred Wegener, in a book published in 1915.
sentences.translation: 大陆正在移动这一观点，最早由德国气象学家阿尔弗雷德·魏格纳在1915年出版的一本书中提出。

structure.text: The idea that the continents are moving
that the continents are moving
was first proposed by a German meteorologist,.Alfred Wegener, in a book published in 1915

difficult_points: that 从句说明 idea 的具体内容，是同位语从句。
源文 meteorologist,.Alfred 含异常标点，语义上 Alfred Wegener 是 meteorologist 的同位语；published... 修饰 book。

sentences.sentence: He had gathered a great deal of careful and tantalising evidence, the most obvious being the simple observation that the great landmasses of the world seem to fit together, jigsaw-like, a striking example being the coastlines of either side of the Atlantic ocean.
sentences.translation: 他收集了大量严谨而又引人遐想的证据，其中最明显的一点是：世界各大块陆地似乎像拼图一样能够拼合；大西洋两岸的海岸线就是一个鲜明例子。

structure.text: He had gathered a great deal of careful and tantalising evidence
the most obvious being the simple observation that the great landmasses of the world seem to fit together, jigsaw-like
that the great landmasses of the world seem to fit together
a striking example being the coastlines of either side of the Atlantic ocean

difficult_points: 两个独立主格结构都用 being 补充说明 evidence，句子主干是 He had gathered...。
that 从句解释 observation；jigsaw-like 表示像拼图一样。

sentences.sentence: Wegener was even able to theorise, correctly, that all the continents were once assembled into a supercontinent (now called Pangaea).
sentences.translation: 魏格纳甚至正确推断，所有大陆曾经聚合成一块超级大陆（如今称为盘古大陆）。

structure.text: Wegener was even able to theorise
correctly
that all the continents were once assembled into a supercontinent (now called Pangaea)
now called Pangaea

difficult_points: that 从句是 theorise 的宾语；correctly 是插入式评注。
were assembled 使用被动语态；now called Pangaea 修饰 supercontinent。

sentences.sentence: Pangaea broke up into Laurasia(which became North America and Eurasia) and Gondwana (which became the remaining continents).
sentences.translation: 盘古大陆分裂成劳亚大陆（后来成为北美洲和欧亚大陆）与冈瓦纳大陆（后来成为其余各大陆）。

structure.text: Pangaea
broke up into Laurasia(which became North America and Eurasia) and Gondwana (which became the remaining continents)
which became North America and Eurasia
which became the remaining continents

difficult_points: 两个 which 从句分别修饰 Laurasia 和 Gondwana。
源文 Laurasia 与左括号之间缺少空格，英文原样保留。

sentences.sentence: Unfortunately, Wegener could propose no propulsive force for this movement, apart from the vague and erroneous suggestion that it might be centrifugal forces.
sentences.translation: 遗憾的是，魏格纳无法为这种运动提出任何推动力，除了一项含糊且错误的猜测：推动力可能是离心力。

structure.text: Unfortunately
Wegener could propose no propulsive force for this movement
apart from the vague and erroneous suggestion that it might be centrifugal forces
that it might be centrifugal forces

difficult_points: apart from 引出唯一例外；that 从句说明 suggestion 的内容。
it 指大陆运动的推动力，而非 movement 本身。

sentences.sentence: He also severely overestimated the speed of this motion.
sentences.translation: 他还严重高估了这种运动的速度。

structure.text: He
also severely overestimated
the speed of this motion

difficult_points: He 指 Wegener，this motion 指大陆移动。
severely 修饰 overestimated，强调高估幅度很大。

sentences.sentence: These problems, and the fact that he was a meteorologist (rather than a geologist), meant that, upon publishing his ideas, the scientific community was resolutely and implacably hostile.
sentences.translation: 这些问题，再加上他是气象学家而非地质学家这一事实，意味着他的观点发表后，科学界对其态度坚决而毫不妥协地敌视。

structure.text: These problems, and the fact that he was a meteorologist (rather than a geologist)
that he was a meteorologist (rather than a geologist)
meant
that, upon publishing his ideas, the scientific community was resolutely and implacably hostile

difficult_points: 主语由 These problems 与 the fact... 并列，第一处 that 是同位语从句。
upon publishing... 表示“在发表之后”；其逻辑主语按语境应是 he，表达略有悬垂。

sentences.sentence: It is an interesting example of that not uncommon instance in which a scientist who was fundamentally correct was denied any recognition in his lifetime.
sentences.translation: 这是一个颇有意思的例子，体现了一种并不少见的情形：一位根本观点正确的科学家，在有生之年却得不到任何认可。

structure.text: It is an interesting example of that not uncommon instance
in which a scientist who was fundamentally correct was denied any recognition in his lifetime
who was fundamentally correct

difficult_points: in which 修饰 instance，描述这种情形的具体表现。
who 修饰 a scientist；was denied 是双宾语结构 deny someone recognition 的被动形式。

sentences.sentence: Semmelweis, who advocated the washing of hands before surgery as a way to reduce hospital fatalities, is another example.
sentences.translation: 塞麦尔维斯是另一个例子；他曾倡导术前洗手，以此降低医院死亡人数。

structure.text: Semmelweis
who advocated the washing of hands before surgery as a way to reduce hospital fatalities
is another example

difficult_points: who 引导非限制性定语从句，补充塞麦尔维斯的主张。
as a way to... 表示把术前洗手视为降低死亡人数的方法。

sentences.sentence: Wegener was to unexpectedly die on an expedition in Greenland, probably of a heart attack - in his death, as in his life, left out in the cold.
sentences.translation: 魏格纳后来在格陵兰考察途中意外去世，很可能死于心脏病——无论生前还是死后，他都被冷落在外。

structure.text: Wegener was to unexpectedly die on an expedition in Greenland
probably of a heart attack
in his death, as in his life, left out in the cold

difficult_points: was to die 在叙事中表示后来注定发生的事，并非计划去世。
left out in the cold 既呼应格陵兰严寒，也比喻他生前死后均受冷落。

sentences.sentence: The first hints of the existence of Gondwana came from the similarity of fossil plants and animals distributed in the same geological period over South America, Africa, Antarctica, India, and Australia.
sentences.translation: 冈瓦纳大陆存在的最初线索，来自南美洲、非洲、南极洲、印度和澳大利亚分布着同一地质时期的相似动植物化石。

structure.text: The first hints of the existence of Gondwana
came from the similarity of fossil plants and animals
distributed in the same geological period over South America, Africa, Antarctica, India, and Australia

difficult_points: 句子主干是 hints came from similarity。
distributed... 修饰 fossil plants and animals，说明这些化石的时代与地理分布。

sentences.sentence: Similarly, the composition and nature of the rocks along relevant coastlines spoke the same story, yet to become scientifically credible, the theory needed evidence of a propulsive force to move such huge continents (in the same way that Semmelweis's ideas needed the germ theory of disease).
sentences.translation: 同样，相关海岸线沿岸岩石的成分和性质也讲述着同一个故事；然而，要让该理论在科学上可信，还需要证明存在能够推动如此巨大大陆的力量——就像塞麦尔维斯的观点需要疾病的细菌理论来支撑一样。

structure.text: the composition and nature of the rocks along relevant coastlines spoke the same story
yet to become scientifically credible
the theory needed evidence of a propulsive force to move such huge continents
in the same way that Semmelweis's ideas needed the germ theory of disease

difficult_points: spoke the same story 是拟人表达，意为岩石证据指向同一结论。
to become... 表示目的；that 从句修饰 way，构成与塞麦尔维斯案例的类比。

sentences.sentence: It was only in the 1960s, decades after Wegener's death, that hard evidence for his theory began amassing to eventually become overwhelming.
sentences.translation: 直到20世纪60年代，也就是魏格纳去世数十年后，支持其理论的确凿证据才开始不断积累，并最终变得无可辩驳。

structure.text: It was only in the 1960s, decades after Wegener's death, that hard evidence for his theory began amassing
only in the 1960s, decades after Wegener's death
that hard evidence for his theory began amassing
to eventually become overwhelming

difficult_points: It was...that... 是强调句，强调证据开始积累的时间。
began amassing 表示证据开始汇集；overwhelming 指证据强大到无法反驳。

sentences.sentence: The theory is now called 'plate tectonics', since it was proven that the Earth's surface is fractured into 'plates'.
sentences.translation: 该理论如今称为“板块构造论”，因为地球表面已被证实分裂成若干“板块”。

structure.text: The theory is now called 'plate tectonics'
since it was proven that the Earth's surface is fractured into 'plates'
that the Earth's surface is fractured into 'plates'

difficult_points: since 引导原因状语从句。
it 是形式主语，that 从句是真正主语；is fractured 强调地表处于分裂状态。

sentences.sentence: These bump and grind as they steadily move at infinitesimally slow rates in given directions, driven by 'convention forces'.
sentences.translation: 这些板块在以极其缓慢的速度沿特定方向稳定移动时彼此碰撞摩擦，而这种运动由“convention forces”驱动。

structure.text: These bump and grind
as they steadily move at infinitesimally slow rates in given directions
driven by 'convention forces'

difficult_points: These 指 plates；bump and grind 是并列动词，描述板块碰撞和摩擦。
driven by... 的逻辑主语是板块；源文 convention forces 疑为 convection forces（对流力），但英文保留原样。

sentences.sentence: These are formed by the vast circular rising of superheated rock from the planet's molten interior.
sentences.translation: 这些力量由过热岩石从地球熔融内部大规模循环上升而形成。

structure.text: These
are formed by
the vast circular rising of superheated rock from the planet's molten interior

difficult_points: These 回指上一句的 forces。
rising 在此是名词，of superheated rock 说明上升的物质，from... 说明来源。

sentences.sentence: This material cools as it nears the surface, eventually sinking once again towards the centre.
sentences.translation: 这种物质接近地表时会冷却，最终再次向地心下沉。

structure.text: This material cools
as it nears the surface
eventually sinking once again towards the centre

difficult_points: This material 指上句的 superheated rock。
as 表示“随着/当……时”；sinking... 描述冷却后的后续过程。

sentences.sentence: Add to this the rotation of the Earth itself, and there is a complicated and barely understood set of cyclic swirls of molten rock, producing drags and pulls on each tectonic plate, the sum of which results in a steady migration.
sentences.translation: 再把地球自身的转动考虑进去，就会形成一套复杂且人们知之甚少的熔岩循环涡流，对每个构造板块产生拖拽和拉力；这些力的合力最终造成板块持续迁移。

structure.text: Add to this the rotation of the Earth itself
and there is a complicated and barely understood set of cyclic swirls of molten rock
producing drags and pulls on each tectonic plate
the sum of which results in a steady migration

difficult_points: “祈使句 + and + 陈述句”表示条件与结果，相当于“如果再加上……，就……”。
which 指 drags and pulls，the sum of which 表示这些力的合力。

sentences.sentence: Of course, this motion is slow, typically at the speed at which fingernails grow, and at its fastest, the rate at which hair does.
sentences.translation: 当然，这种运动很缓慢，通常只有指甲生长的速度；即使最快，也不过是头发生长的速度。

structure.text: this motion is slow
typically at the speed at which fingernails grow
and at its fastest, the rate at which hair does

difficult_points: 两个 at which 定语从句分别修饰 speed 和 rate。
hair does 中 does 代替 grows，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: But by being consistent and essentially unstoppable, the results can be spectacular, particularly when plates meet.
sentences.translation: 但由于这种运动持续不断且基本无法阻挡，它造成的结果可能极其壮观，尤其是在板块相遇时。

structure.text: by being consistent and essentially unstoppable
the results can be spectacular
particularly when plates meet

difficult_points: by being... 表示原因或实现方式，其逻辑对象是前文的 motion。
when plates meet 是时间从句，说明壮观结果尤其发生于何时。

sentences.sentence: Here, the release of heat, as well as the buckling and melting which results, gives rise to geological events such as earthquakes, and geological features such as mountains, volcanoes, and oceanic ridges and trenches.
sentences.translation: 在这里，热量释放以及由此产生的弯曲和熔化，会引发地震等地质事件，并形成山脉、火山、海岭和海沟等地质地貌。

structure.text: the release of heat, as well as the buckling and melting which results
which results
gives rise to geological events such as earthquakes, and geological features such as mountains, volcanoes, and oceanic ridges and trenches

difficult_points: as well as 连接附加成分，谓语与首个中心词 release 一致，使用 gives。
which results 修饰 buckling and melting；give rise to 后并列 events 与 features。

sentences.sentence: Plate boundaries see most of the world's active volcanoes, with the Pacific Plate's 'Ring of Fire' being a good example.
sentences.translation: 世界上大多数活火山都出现在板块边界，太平洋板块的“火环”就是一个典型例子。

structure.text: Plate boundaries see most of the world's active volcanoes
with the Pacific Plate's 'Ring of Fire' being a good example

difficult_points: see 在此为拟人用法，实际表示板块边界是活火山集中出现之处。
with...being... 是 with 复合结构，用例子补充说明主句。

sentences.sentence: Volcanism may sometimes occur in the middle of plates, but this has been theorised to be a result of 'hotspots': anomalously hot areas of interior rock which melt through the plate, forcing an escape to the surface.
sentences.translation: 火山活动有时也会发生在板块中央，但理论认为，这是“热点”造成的结果：内部岩层中温度异常高的区域熔穿板块，迫使熔融物质逸出地表。

structure.text: Volcanism may sometimes occur in the middle of plates
but this has been theorised to be a result of 'hotspots'
anomalously hot areas of interior rock which melt through the plate
forcing an escape to the surface

difficult_points: this 指板块中央发生火山活动的现象；冒号后解释 hotspots。
which 修饰 areas；forcing... 表示熔穿板块所造成的结果。

sentences.sentence: Plate boundaries come in three types.
sentences.translation: 板块边界分为三种类型。

structure.text: Plate boundaries
come in three types

difficult_points: come in 在分类语境中表示“有……种类型”。
three types 为下文三类板块边界的总起。

sentences.sentence: First, Transform boundaries, where the plates grind past each other.
sentences.translation: 第一种是转换型边界，板块在这里彼此擦过。

structure.text: First
Transform boundaries
where the plates grind past each other

difficult_points: 这是省略句，Transform boundaries 前省略了 there are。
where 引导定语从句修饰 boundaries；grind past 表示相互摩擦着错开。

sentences.sentence: It was once thought that the well-known Aegir Ridge was an example, until studies showed that it had never been active, whereas the periodic earthquakes along California's San Andreas Fault show the very opposite case.
sentences.translation: 人们一度认为著名的埃吉尔海岭就是一个例子，直到研究表明它从未活动过；相比之下，加利福尼亚圣安德烈亚斯断层沿线周期性发生的地震恰好说明了相反情况。

structure.text: It was once thought that the well-known Aegir Ridge was an example
until studies showed that it had never been active
whereas the periodic earthquakes along California's San Andreas Fault show the very opposite case

difficult_points: 首个 It 是形式主语，that 从句承载被认为的内容。
until 表示旧观点持续到研究推翻它为止；whereas 引出圣安德烈亚斯断层的鲜明对比。

sentences.sentence: The second type is Divergent boundaries, where the two plates slide apart from each other.
sentences.translation: 第二种是离散型边界，两个板块在这里彼此分离滑动。

structure.text: The second type is Divergent boundaries
where the two plates slide apart from each other

difficult_points: where 引导非限制性定语从句，说明 Divergent boundaries 的运动方式。
slide apart from each other 强调两个板块相背分开。

sentences.sentence: Mid-oceanic ridges, such as in the Atlantic, and active rift zones, such as in East Africa, are examples.
sentences.translation: 大西洋等地的洋中脊，以及东非等地的活跃裂谷带，都是这类边界的例子。

structure.text: Mid-oceanic ridges, such as in the Atlantic, and active rift zones, such as in East Africa
are examples

difficult_points: 主语由 Mid-oceanic ridges 与 active rift zones 两项并列。
两个 such as in... 分别举出对应地理实例。

sentences.sentence: Finally, there are Convergent boundaries, where the two plates slide towards each other.
sentences.translation: 最后一种是汇聚型边界，两个板块在这里相向滑动。

structure.text: Finally
there are Convergent boundaries
where the two plates slide towards each other

difficult_points: there are 引出第三种边界类型。
where 修饰 Convergent boundaries；towards each other 表示相向运动。

sentences.sentence: This can form either a subduction zone (if one plate moves underneath the other) or a continental collision.
sentences.translation: 这种运动既可能形成俯冲带（一个板块移动到另一个板块下方），也可能造成大陆碰撞。

structure.text: This
can form either a subduction zone (if one plate moves underneath the other) or a continental collision
if one plate moves underneath the other

difficult_points: This 指两个板块相向运动；either...or... 并列两种结果。
if 从句解释形成 subduction zone 的条件，the other 指另一个板块。

sentences.sentence: Deep marine trenches are formed in the former case, and with the descending plate releasing its trapped water on being heated in the Earth's interior, huge amounts of heat and pressure rise to the surface, causing mountains and volcanoes to form, such as in the Andes mountain range.
sentences.translation: 在前一种情况下会形成深海沟；与此同时，下沉板块在地球内部受热时释放其内部水分，大量热量和压力由此升至地表，促使山脉和火山形成，安第斯山脉便是例子。

structure.text: Deep marine trenches are formed in the former case
with the descending plate releasing its trapped water on being heated in the Earth's interior
huge amounts of heat and pressure rise to the surface
causing mountains and volcanoes to form

difficult_points: the former case 指前句两种结果中的 subduction zone。
with...releasing... 是独立结构；on being heated 表示“一受热”。
causing... 表示热量和压力上升所带来的结果。

sentences.sentence: The best example of a continental collision is the Indian plate, which is steadily and implacably migrating straight into central Asia.
sentences.translation: 大陆碰撞的最佳例子是印度板块，它正持续且不可阻挡地径直向中亚推进。

structure.text: The best example of a continental collision is the Indian plate
which is steadily and implacably migrating straight into central Asia

difficult_points: which 指 Indian plate，引导非限制性定语从句。
steadily and implacably 同时修饰 migrating，强调持续且无法阻挡。

sentences.sentence: The Himalayas of Nepal and Northern India, the Karakoram Ranges of Northern Pakistan, and the highlands of Afghanistan, are all part of the complex fold system that resulted, producing some of the highest peaks in the world.
sentences.translation: 尼泊尔和印度北部的喜马拉雅山脉、巴基斯坦北部的喀喇昆仑山脉以及阿富汗高地，都是由此形成的复杂褶皱体系的一部分，并造就了世界上一些最高峰。

structure.text: The Himalayas of Nepal and Northern India, the Karakoram Ranges of Northern Pakistan, and the highlands of Afghanistan
are all part of the complex fold system that resulted
that resulted
producing some of the highest peaks in the world

difficult_points: 三个地貌名称构成长主语，谓语是 are。
that resulted 修饰 fold system；producing... 表示该褶皱体系造成的结果。

sentences.sentence: There are also some deep valleys receiving the run-off melt-water from the far side of these mountains, creating some mighty rivers, such as the Indus, the Irrawaddy, and the Mekong.
sentences.translation: 这里还有一些深谷，接纳群山另一侧流下的融水，从而汇成印度河、伊洛瓦底江和湄公河等浩荡河流。

structure.text: There are also some deep valleys
receiving the run-off melt-water from the far side of these mountains
creating some mighty rivers
such as the Indus, the Irrawaddy, and the Mekong

difficult_points: receiving... 修饰 valleys，说明深谷接纳的水源。
creating... 表示由融水汇流产生河流的结果；such as 后列举河流。

sentences.sentence: Interestingly, the Himalayas are still growing, meaning that the summit of Mount Everest is perhaps a couple of metres higher now than when people first stood there in 1953, presumably making it just that little bit harder to reach.
sentences.translation: 有趣的是，喜马拉雅山脉仍在升高；这意味着珠穆朗玛峰峰顶如今可能比1953年人类首次登顶时高出几米，因此抵达峰顶大概也就更困难了一点。

structure.text: Interestingly, the Himalayas are still growing
meaning that the summit of Mount Everest is perhaps a couple of metres higher now than when people first stood there in 1953
when people first stood there in 1953
presumably making it just that little bit harder to reach

difficult_points: meaning... 与 making... 是连续的结果性分词短语，后者承接峰顶变高这一事实。
than 后的 when 从句提供比较基准；there 指珠峰峰顶。
making it...harder to reach 中 it 指 summit，to reach 与 harder 搭配。

expressions.text: a little bit harder to reach
